rationale: X's suggestion to derive the component list from a single source of truth (echoing dsocek's actual review comment about generalizing the PEFT-name mapping instead of hardcoding) gives it meaningful recall on the one substantive ground-truth concern; it also surfaces a plausible, actionable follow-up (text-encoder LoRA silent no-op) with concrete file:line and fix suggestions. Y's review is accurate but consists mostly of verification notes confirming the diff is correct, surfacing zero of the reviewer concerns and offering no actionable next steps, landing at plain APPROVE like the ground-truth LGTMs but without the substance. Both are well-grounded with no fabrications, but X is clearly more useful.
